name: County Cork Irish Stew
recipeInstructions:
1. Season the lamb chops with salt and pepper.
2. Heat oil in a wide saucepan that can hold all chops in a single layer.
3. Brown the chops on both sides. Remove any melted fat.
4. Add enough water to cover the chops, then bring to a boil.
5. Lower heat and add parsley, bay leaves, peppercorns, thyme, and rosemary (packed in cheesecloth).
6. While heating the liquid, peel potatoes and cut into bite-sized rounds.
7. Chop any potato trimmings into small pieces.
8. Add the chopped potatoes, potato trimmings, shredded cabbage, onion, leek, white onions, and celery to the pot with chops and liquid.
9. Simmer for 20 minutes, then add peas.
10. If needed, add a little more water during cooking.
11. Continue simmering for an additional 10 minutes or until potatoes are tender.
12. Adjust seasoning if necessary and garnish with parsley before serving.
